translation.literal_translation: And because of mention of the path of one who has heard the Upanishadic secret. (16)
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.16: shrutopanishatka-gati attribution indicates Brahman, not a lower referent.
translation.interpretive_note: Sutra shorthand for the devayana-identification argument.

lexical_notes.gloss: path taught in revealed esoteric Upanishadic instruction
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Sutra reason: the described post-mortem trajectory is specific to Brahmavid contexts.
Path designation functions as linga to identify the referent as Brahman.
The sutra concludes that only Brahman matches the path-profile in canonical usage.
Compact reason structure condenses broader bhashya argument into one decisive sign.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit antaryamin technical vocabulary and akshi-purusha predicate nuances in records 1.2.040-1.2.046.
